steps.title: Identify sides relative to angle C
steps.content: In right triangle ABC with the right angle at B, angle C is at the lower right corner. From angle C's perspective: the opposite side is AB (vertical), the adjacent side is BC (horizontal), and the hypotenuse AC is given as 90.

steps.title: Set up the tangent equation
steps.content:
The tangent ratio for angle C is:
$$\tan(C) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{AB}{BC} = \frac{3}{4}$$
This means AB and BC are in the ratio 3:4, so we can write $AB = 3m$ and $BC = 4m$ for some positive constant $m$.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
The sides must satisfy the Pythagorean theorem:
$$(3m)^2 + (4m)^2 = 90^2$$
$$9m^2 + 16m^2 = 8100$$
$$25m^2 = 8100$$
$$m^2 = 324$$
$$m = 18$$

steps.title: Calculate AB
steps.content:
Now that we know $m = 18$, we can find the length of $\overline{AB}$:
$$AB = 3m = 3(18) = 54$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
